title: BBC Radio 1 Warns: Don't Buy Tickets from Touts
summary: BBC Radio 1 is having a big music party called the Big Weekend. Tickets cost £45 for Saturday and Sunday. But some people are buying many tickets and selling them for a lot more money, like £622! This is called touting. Radio 1 says if you buy tickets from touts, you might not be allowed into the party. They want you to buy tickets only from the official website or Ticketmaster. The government wants to make a new law to stop people from selling tickets for too much money.

keywords.explanation: people who buy tickets and sell them for more money
the original price of a ticket
when someone lies to get money

questions.question: How much does a ticket cost at first?
What might happen if you buy from a tout?
What does the government want to do?

background_read: The Big Weekend is a free music festival organized by BBC Radio 1. It started in 2000 and takes place in different cities each year. The festival features popular singers and bands. Touting is when people buy tickets just to sell them for more money. This makes it hard for real fans to get tickets.
Article_Structure: WHO: BBC Radio 1 and FanFair Alliance
WHAT: Warning against buying tickets from touts
WHY: To protect fans from high prices and fraud

perspectives.description: Wants fans to buy only from official sellers to avoid problems.
Thinks touts are unfair and wants a new law to stop them.
